title: French PM Lecornu warns of 'fairly severe' wildfires arriving unusually early
subhead: According to the prime minister, the wildfire season is running at least two weeks ahead of its usual schedule. With vegetation already extremely dry, he voiced concerns about the 'endurance' of firefighting resources.

title: Trump, revisionism and the citizens fighting for the truth

France's independent cinema chain MK2 bets on crowdfunding – and not corporate money – for its renovation
subhead: To bring its venues up to energy standards and help transform the biggest of its 10 Paris locations, directors Elisha and Nathanaël Karmitz are turning to the public.

title: French justice system investigates alleged criminal Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon
subhead: A Franco-Lebanese national has filed a complaint in Paris for war crimes and crimes against humanity after four members of his family were killed in a bombing in Tyre in April. This is the second such complaint filed in France regarding Israeli military operations in Lebanon.

title: Spain power into World Cup last 16 with 3-0 win over Austria
subhead: The European champions controlled possession and sliced through the Austrian defense in a typically dominant performance in Los Angeles. The win sets up a tantalizing round-of-16 clash with either Portugal or Croatia.

title: Anthropic President Daniela Amodei: 'We must embrace both the positive side and the dark side of AI'
subhead: The president of the American artificial intelligence start-up, valued at $965 billion and known for its warnings about the risks of this technology, shares her perspective with Le Monde on the major issues facing the sector.

A new radical left is shaking up the Democratic party
subhead: A new generation of candidates, critical of elites, social injustice and US support for Israel, is making its mark in the Democratic primaries ahead of November's midterm elections.

title: Lebanon's Shiites, torn over Hezbollah, question the meaning of their sacrifices
subhead: Days after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement, supporters and opponents of the militia-party are still measuring the consequences of the devastating war.

title: In the DRC's Ebola epicenter, a 'cascade of deaths' and fear of contamination
subhead: Le Monde traveled to Mongbwalu, a town in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, where the Ebola virus outbreak began. As the death toll continues to rise, local health facilities and NGOs have become overwhelmed, hampered by a shortage of resources.
byline: Morgane Le Cam and  Philémon Barbier
